items.title: Conceição: No obligation to pass ball to Ronaldo
items.summary: Portugal's Francisco Conceição on Sunday rejected suggestions that the team feels compelled to constantly look for Cristiano Ronaldo on the pitch at this 2026 FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Japan set sights on WCup glory after record win
items.summary: After a record-breaking evening for Japan, Daichi Kamada reiterated their target of winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Messi, Haaland, Mbappé, Kane: Who will be the Worl...
items.summary: We're more than a week into the 2026 World Cup, and the race for the Golden Boot -- given to the tournament's top goal scorer -- is heating up.

items.title: The World Cup records that look set to be broken
items.summary: The 2026 World Cup may only be 10 days old but the tournament has already rewritten football history. BBC Sport takes a look at the records already broken, or set to be.

items.title: Lamine Yamal is Spain's main man - Rooney analysis
items.summary: Watch BBC pundits Wayne Rooney, Cesar Azpilicueta, and Thomas Frank give their analysis on Lamine Yamal's performance for Spain against Saudi Arabia in the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: 'He's arrived' - Lamine Yamal scores the opener for Spain
items.summary: Spain's Lamine Yamal scores the opening goal against Saudi Arabia in their Group H match in the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: How to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
items.summary: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

items.title: Goalline technology denies Japan goal by fractions
items.summary: Tunisia goalkeeper Aymen Dahmen makes an "unbelievable save" to prevent Japan from doubling their lead by the finest of margins, in their 2026 World Cup match at Estadio Monterrey.

items.title: Japan run riot as Tunisia eliminated in Renard's first game
items.summary: Ayase Ueda scores twice to help Japan to a thumping 4-0 win, as Herve Renard's first game in charge of Tunisia ends with his side being eliminated from the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: World Cup first as Almiron sent off for covering mouth
items.summary: Watch Miguel Almiron become the first player to be sent off under new rules preventing players from covering their mouths during a game at the 2026 World Cup, with the Paraguay midfielder shown red in a win over Turkey.

items.title: Why are footballers wearing pink boots at the World Cup?

items.title: Uruguay v Cape Verde: World Cup 2026 – live
items.summary:
⚽️ Kick-off: 6pm local time/11pm BST/8am (Mon) AEST
⚽️ Player guide | Bracketology | Golden Boot | Email Beau

We’re off. Uruguay in their pleasant blue and white. Cape Verde in all red.

I very much like the Cape Verde national anthem. I’ll see if my band can open our next show with it, though this rendition has the odd tempo change that would probably throw us off.

Continue reading...

items.title: The 2026 World Cup team of the tournament so far (without the superstars)
items.summary:
We pick an XI of players who have impressed during the initial rounds of games in Canada, the US and Mexico

A star was born, at 40, when a player whose highest-profile employers were Portugal’s Gil Vicente denied Spain’s all-stars in that historic 0-0 draw. His Christian name being Josimar may well have pointed to his being a nascent World Cup cult hero. So huge was his impact that the US authorities, on the orders of the House Democratic leader, Hakeem Jeffries, waived the visa fee and $15,000 (£11,300) bond for his mother, now able to fly in for her boy’s continuing adventures. Seven saves from Spain have made him a global social media sensation, too.

Continue reading...
